TNT's Ernie Johnson said near the end of TNT's Inside the NBA broadcast on Tuesday night that Robert Covington's nose injury isn't something that will necessarily keep him out of Thursday's Game 4.
EJ mentioned that Jared Greenberg was able to get information that Covington might be available for Game 4, which is great news, especially since the Rockets may still be without Danuel House (personal) for that one. Mike D'Antoni said after the game that Covington "got hit in the nose. He was bleeding pretty good," and that the Rockets don't know his status for Game 4 yet. This is all we know so we're not going to speculate further, but we should have a fresh and more detailed report out of the bubble on Wednesday. The bottom line is that it does sound like Covington at least has a chance to play in Game 4, which is great news given how nasty the collision looked on television.
